title: elaeocarpus ferrugineus subsp. elliptifolius
intro: Elaeocarpus ferrugineus subsp. elliptifolius is a subspecies of Elaeocarpus ferrugineus, a type of flowering plant native to Australia, often referred to as a blue berry ash tree. The medicinal properties of this plant are primarily attributed to its leaves, which have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions. The leaves of Elaeocarpus ferrugineus subsp. elliptifolius have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
